title: Xtra Pillowy Vietnamese Ground Cardamom Bun Bo Kho
description: A show-off Vietnamese dish featuring ground cardamom in a rich, pillowy, and aromatic bo kho broth. This recipe is suitable for shojin-friendly diets.
instructions: In a large pot, combine the beef bones, onion, garlic, and ginger, and pour in 4 cups of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 1 hour.
Add the fish sauce, soy sauce, sugar, star anise, and cinnamon to the pot, and continue to simmer for another 30 minutes.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
In a small bowl, mix together the ground cardamom and 1 tsp of sugar.
Add the cardamom mixture to the broth and stir to combine.
To make the rice pillow, cook the rice according to package instructions and mix it with 2 tbsp of lime juice and 1 tsp of salt.
Mold the rice mixture into small balls and flatten them slightly into disks.
In a large skillet or wok, heat 2 tbsp of oil over medium heat.
Add the bean sprouts and scallions to the skillet and stir-fry until they are tender.
Add the fish cake to the skillet and stir-fry until it is lightly browned.
Add the rice pillows to the skillet and stir-fry until they are lightly browned on both sides.
To serve, place a few rice pillows on each plate, then ladle the hot broth over the top.
Garnish with additional bean sprouts and scallions if desired.
